text:
I. THE COMMUNIST BLOC

Communist China - Indonesia: Chinese Communist Premier Chou En-lai has hinted at economic retaliation, both by Peiping and by Overseas Chinese in Indonesia, in an effort to force Djakarta to "delay" on plans to force Overseas Chinese merchants out of rural areas. During Subandrio's recent discussions in Peiping, Chou made "subtle threats" that China could obtain raw materials through Singapore rather than from Indonesia. Although this is in fact an insignificant threat in view of China's relatively small purchases from Indonesia, Peiping could stall on implementing economic aid and withdraw offers of military aid.

Subandrio's strong impressions of Communist China's growing industrial and military power reinforced his view that Indonesia must follow a policy of nonalignment
